channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - www.gamedeveloper.com
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for www.gamedeveloper.com

item.title: Luna Abyss development team laid off by Kwalee
item.content:encoded:
Chris Kerr,

Senior Editor, News,GameDeveloper.com

June 17, 2026

1 Min Read

UK publisher Kwalee has laid off the development team behind Luna Abyss less than a month after it hit shelves. 

The bullet hell shooter launched on May 21, 2026, and was developed by Kwalee Labs, which was formerly known as Bonsai Collective before being acquired by Kwalee through administration in September 2025. 

A LinkedIn post from studio CEO Hollie Emery explains the entire nine-person team has been laid off.

"We're enamoured by the love and support it received both by our industry and critically by journalists and media. Whilst we faced many challenges along the way, it has been the highlight of our careers—and we are incredibly proud that it has finally seen the light of day (thank you to everyone who believed in us!)," wrote Emery. 

"Unfortunately as of yesterday, the entire team has been made redundant; a decision that was completely outside of our control. As a result, the entire team are available for work as of today."

Related:Treyarch studio head steps down after 22 years

Kwalee has published over 70 titles across PC, console, and mobile that have collectively amassed over 30 million monthly active users.

Game Developer has reached out to the company for comment.

About the Author

Senior Editor, News, GameDeveloper.com

Game Developer news editor Chris Kerr is an award-winning reporter with over a decade of experience in the game industry. His byline has appeared in notable print and digital publications including Edge, Stuff, Wireframe, International Business Times, and PocketGamer.biz. Throughout his career, Chris has covered major industry events including GDC, PAX Australia, Gamescom, Paris Games Week, and Develop Brighton. 



https://www.gamedeveloper.com/business/luna-abyss-development-team-laid-off-by-kwalee
